title: [Bug]: Unexpected result under repeatable read isolation level with consistent snapshot
description:
<!-- A clear and concise description of bug. -->
When I tested the database, I found a problem.
As in the example below, with repeatable read isolation level, transaction 2 reads the update from transaction 1. In my opinion, transaction 2 shouldn't read because the snapshot was created at the beginning of the transaction and it can only see its own snapshot.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE tTest (ID INT PRIMARY KEY, VAL INT, c0 INT UNSIGNED NOT NULL, c1 VARCHAR(100) DEFAULT '', c2 BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE);
INSERT INTO tTest (ID, VAL, c0, c1, c2) VALUES (1, 100, 5000, 'test1', TRUE);
INSERT INTO tTest (ID, VAL, c0, c1, c2) VALUES (2, 200, 6000, 'test2', FALSE);
SET SESSION TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ;
key_bug_features: Transaction reads updates outside its snapshot visibility
